Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Understand the Goal The objective is to analyze a provided event log for a Procure to Pay P2P process identify anomalies deviations from standard best practices and explain why they are problematic

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Understand the Standard P2P Process Before looking at the data I need to establish the Happy Path or standard flow for Procure to Pay

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Key Dependencies Constraints PR must be approved before PO is issued PO must be issued before Goods are received usually or at least before Invoice Matching Goods should ideally be received before Payment unless advance payment is agreed but standard P2P assumes GR first Invoice should be matched before Payment Approval must happen before Commitment PO

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 1001 Create PR Approve PR Issue PO Receive Goods Invoice Received Match Invoice Release Payment Close Sequence Logical PR approved before PO PO before Goods Goods before Payment Match before Payment Verdict Normal Compliant

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 1002 Anomaly 1 PO Issued before PR Approval The PO was sent to the vendor before internal management approved the spend Anomaly 2 Payment Released before PR Approval Payment happened before Approval paying before internal approval is a major control failure Anomaly 3 Payment Released before Goods Received While advance payments exist in standard P2P this is risky paying for nothing Anomaly 4 Payment Released before Invoice Matching You paid before verifying the invoice matches the PO Goods Verdict Highly Anomalous High Risk

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 1003 Anomaly 1 Payment Released before Invoice Received You cannot match an invoice you have not received Anomaly 2 Payment Released before Goods Received Paying before getting the items Anomaly 3 Payment Released before Matching Matching happens after Payment defeats the purpose of matching Verdict Anomalous High Risk

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: Looks like an unauthorized advance payment or data entry error

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 1004 Anomaly 1 Goods Received before PO Issued This is Maverick Buying The department got the goods before formally ordering them This undermines the negotiation and approval power of the PO

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The PO was issued 15 minutes after goods receipt This suggests retroactive documentation to cover up the unauthorized purchase

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Start with a brief definition of the standard P2P flow to set the baseline Go case by case skipping the normal one or mentioning it briefly as a baseline Highlight specific anomalies for the problematic cases Explain the business impact risk for each anomaly for example fraud risk financial loss audit failure Conclude with a summary of findings

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 1002 Make sure to emphasize the approval timestamp It is explicitly after the PO and Payment This is a segregation of duties approval authority violation Case 1003 Emphasize the Payment before Invoice aspect Payment before Invoice Case 1004 Focus on Maverick Buying Receiving goods before a PO exists means the price and terms were not legally locked in before the liability was incurred Case 1001 Explicitly state this is the control baseline

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Self Correction during drafting I should make sure I do not just list the errors but explain why they are problematic as requested I will add a Business Implication section for each anomaly

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Final Polish Check timestamps again to ensure accuracy in the description 1002 Approval is days after PO and Payment 1003 Payment before Invoice 1004 Goods before PO
